analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet lists topics Secretary Pompeo addressed (COVID-19, U.S.-Afghan peace process, ICC, WIPO election, China, Iran). It is informational and neutral, without value judgments or praise. Evaluation by narrative: US1 (honorable tradition) – not promoted; no reference to values or historical legacy. US2 (admirable society) – not promoted; no mention of society, diversity, civil groups, companies, or heroes. US3 (hostile enemies) – not promoted; while China and Iran are mentioned, there is no framing of them as immoral or hostile in this tweet. US4 (force for good) – not promoted; mentioning the U.S.-Afghan peace process could imply peace efforts, but the tweet does not claim U.S. benevolence or support for rights; it merely states topics. US5 (great international ally) – not promoted; no praise of allies or commitments. US6 (at the forefront) – not promoted; no claim of leadership or superiority, only that the Secretary spoke on multiple issues. Conclusion: No listed narratives are explicitly or implicitly promoted.
